topic: Sharp identification phase transition for the average treatment effect via proximal causal inference (negative controls) when standard proxy completeness fails.

open_problems.open_problem: Characterize the sharp proximal ATE identified set under weak proxy completeness by an operator-strength parameter, including exact point, nontrivial-interval, and Manski-trivial regimes.

source_refs.claim: Establishes nonparametric point identification with two proxy variables under rank/completeness-type conditions, but leaves completeness failure outside the point-identification theorem.
source_refs.author: Wang Miao, Zhi Geng, Eric J. Tchetgen Tchetgen

source_refs.claim: Develops proximal ATE efficiency and doubly robust estimation under bridge/completeness conditions, not under a sharp weak-completeness frontier.
source_refs.author: Yifan Cui, Hongming Pu, Xu Shi, Wang Miao, Eric Tchetgen Tchetgen
source_refs.venue: Journal of the American Statistical Association

source_refs.claim: Motivates partial identification because completeness is not empirically testable and lack of completeness can sharply limit bridge and causal-effect identification.
source_refs.author: AmirEmad Ghassami, Ilya Shpitser, Eric Tchetgen Tchetgen

source_refs.excerpt: Pivot to a new angle (e.g. an observable full-fiber realisability criterion

open_problems.why_unsolved: The published proximal ATE results give point identification under completeness and separate partial-identification bounds when completeness is absent, while the banked proxy-mediation attempt shows the danger of simply assuming the whole bridge-null interval is attainable: its full-fiber sharpness claim collapsed to a definitional projection identity. The missing obstacle is an observable operator or finite-projection criterion that proves which residual bridge directions are genuinely attainable and how that attainable set changes as the proxy operator loses singular strength.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A sharp identified-set theorem defining an observable conditional-expectation kernel, deriving a critical sigma_star or proving none exists, and giving exact ATE formulas or counterexamples for the point, interval, and Manski regimes.

open_problems.open_problem: Solve the finite-state rank-deficient negative-control problem: give the exact ATE or mediation-effect identified set as a function of proxy transition matrices, bridge null spaces, and bounded outcome residuals.

source_refs.claim: Develops double-negative-control adjustment with categorical latent-confounding structure, highlighting finite-rank proxy restrictions.
source_refs.author: Xu Shi, Wang Miao, Jennifer C. Nelson, Eric J. Tchetgen Tchetgen
source_refs.venue: Journal of the Royal Statistical Society Series B

source_refs.claim: Provides L2-complete and boundedly-complete distribution examples for econometric identification, but not sharp identified sets across rank-deficient regimes.
source_refs.author: Donald W. K. Andrews

source_refs.claim: Shows many proxy controls can reveal low-rank latent structure, but does not give exact causal-effect intervals indexed by bridge-null residuals.
source_refs.author: Ben Deaner

source_refs.excerpt: failure of proximal completeness has a structured sharp joint identified set

open_problems.why_unsolved: Finite-state proxy models reduce completeness to matrix rank and singular directions, but existing work mainly uses these conditions to recover point identification or estimate low-rank confounding. The prior rank-one proxy-null proposal gives the right finite algebraic substrate, but its sharpness remains conjectural because the affine image of bridge-null boxes may be only an outer set unless a finite response-type realization theorem is proved.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A finite-dimensional linear-algebra and LP theorem giving sharp lower and upper causal-effect bounds from the proxy matrices, null-space loadings, and bounded residual response vectors, with explicit full-rank, rank-deficient, and zero-rank reductions.

open_problems.open_problem: Prove a finite realization theorem turning proxy-envelope LP feasibility into an actual sharp causal model for negative-control partial-identification problems.

source_refs.excerpt: The proof never constructs such a finite model or an observable Pr(A,Y,W,Z)

open_problems.why_unsolved: The prior multi-mediator proxy-envelope reviewer identified the exact obstruction that also threatens proximal phase-transition claims: the LP arithmetic can be correct while sharpness is only assumed, because no finite potential-outcome and proxy distribution is constructed to realize every feasible proxy-envelope array. Without that construction, a computed interval may be a relaxation rather than the true identified set.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A Balke-Pearl-style latent-table realization theorem, at least for rational finite proxy systems, proving that each feasible LP array corresponds to a compatible causal model and hence that the LP projection is sharp.

open_problems.open_problem: Characterize when the proximal ATE linear functional is point identified even though the bridge function is weakly identified or nonunique.

source_refs.claim: Shows bridge-equation solutions may be nonunique and develops inference for a counterfactual mean without imposing uniqueness assumptions.
source_refs.author: Jeffrey Zhang, Wei Li, Wang Miao, Eric Tchetgen Tchetgen

source_refs.claim: Studies inference on continuous linear functionals when nuisance functions from conditional moments are weakly identified or have multiple solutions, including proximal examples.
source_refs.author: Andrew Bennett, Nathan Kallus, Xiaojie Mao, Whitney Newey, Vasilis Syrgkanis, Masatoshi Uehara

source_refs.claim: Develops recovery of continuous linear functionals in NPIV problems, separating functional identification from full structural-function identification.
source_refs.author: Andres Santos

open_problems.why_unsolved: Nonunique-bridge work shows bridge uniqueness is stronger than functional identification, and NPIV work gives adjoint/range tools for linear functionals. What is missing is a proximal-specific necessary-and-sufficient spectral condition that tells when the ATE contrast annihilates all bridge-null directions, or lies in the relevant adjoint range, even as the bridge itself remains nonunique.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A necessary-and-sufficient theorem stating that the ATE loading is invariant over the bridge solution fiber exactly when it belongs to a computable adjoint range or null-annihilator subspace, with examples where bridge nonuniqueness coexists with ATE point identification.

open_problems.open_problem: Determine whether sigma_min and a proximal phase boundary can be honestly diagnosed from observables, or prove an impossibility theorem without extra finite-dimensional structure.

source_refs.claim: Shows no nontrivial tests exist for certain completeness and identification hypotheses in nonparametric endogenous models.
source_refs.author: Ivan A. Canay, Andres Santos, Azeem M. Shaikh

source_refs.claim: Frames proximal inference as leveraging proxies for latent confounding when exchangeability fails, while relying on proxy and bridge assumptions.
source_refs.author: Eric J. Tchetgen Tchetgen, Andrew Ying, Yifan Cui, Xu Shi, Wang Miao

source_refs.claim: Reviews weak-instrument econometrics where conventional inference fails under weak relevance, motivating robust strength diagnostics.
source_refs.author: Isaiah Andrews, James H. Stock, Liyang Sun

open_problems.why_unsolved: A proposed singular-value threshold is only useful if the relevant operator strength is observable or can be bounded under stated restrictions. Completeness is generally non-testable in nonparametric models, so a diagnostic must either impose finite-state/sieve/support conditions that make a lower singular-value bound estimable, or show that uniformly valid data-driven phase classification is impossible.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: Either an honest confidence set for a lower bound on proxy strength under explicit finite-dimensional or sieve restrictions, or a no-go theorem showing that no uniformly valid test can classify the point, interval, and trivial regimes in the unrestricted proximal model.

open_problems.open_problem: Establish local-to-failure asymptotics for proximal OR, IPW, and doubly robust estimators when the bridge operator's smallest singular value drifts to zero.

source_refs.claim: Characterizes proximal doubly robust and locally efficient estimators under identifying bridge conditions.
source_refs.author: Yifan Cui, Hongming Pu, Xu Shi, Wang Miao, Eric Tchetgen Tchetgen
source_refs.venue: Journal of the American Statistical Association

source_refs.claim: Targets bridge estimation with minimax learning and avoids completeness or uniqueness assumptions as much as possible.
source_refs.author: Nathan Kallus, Xiaojie Mao, Masatoshi Uehara

source_refs.claim: Allows penalized nuisance estimators to converge to fixed limits regardless of nuisance identifiability while supporting inference on functionals.
source_refs.author: Andrew Bennett, Nathan Kallus, Xiaojie Mao, Whitney Newey, Vasilis Syrgkanis, Masatoshi Uehara

open_problems.why_unsolved: Semiparametric proximal inference is developed under bridge-identification conditions, while weak-IV asymptotics handles different finite-dimensional relevance failures. A proximal local-to-failure theory must combine ill-posed inverse-problem blow-up, bridge nonuniqueness, and orthogonal-score behavior when the operator's smallest singular value is local to zero.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A sequence-of-experiments theorem with sigma_min,n tending to zero that gives estimator limits, bias and variance rates, weak-proxy-robust confidence sets, and conditions under which OR, IPW, and DR estimators remain valid or fail.

open_problems.open_problem: Separate regularization artifacts from true proximal identification when bridge completeness fails.

source_refs.claim: Treats nonparametric instrumental regression as an ill-posed inverse problem and uses Tikhonov regularization for estimation.
source_refs.author: Serge Darolles, Yanqin Fan, Jean-Pierre Florens, Eric Renault

source_refs.claim: Explains that discontinuous inverse mappings in economic models create ill-posed inverse problems requiring regularization.
source_refs.author: Joel L. Horowitz

source_refs.claim: Shows nonunique proximal bridge solutions complicate estimation and inference, motivating explicit treatment of the solution set.
source_refs.author: Jeffrey Zhang, Wei Li, Wang Miao, Eric Tchetgen Tchetgen

open_problems.why_unsolved: Regularization can select a stable pseudoinverse bridge even when the underlying causal parameter is only partially identified. Existing proximal estimation papers do not fully classify when the regularized limit is invariant over the sharp identified set versus when different penalties select different causal values outside what the data identify.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A theorem comparing regularized bridge limits to the sharp proximal identified set, with necessary and sufficient conditions for regularization-invariant ATEs and counterexamples where two admissible regularizers converge to different causal values.

literature_map:
1. Core proximal point identification: Miao-Geng-Tchetgen (2018, Biometrika) gives proxy-based nonparametric ATE identification under rank/completeness; Cui-Pu-Shi-Miao-Tchetgen (2024, JASA) adds semiparametric efficiency and DR estimators.
2. Proximal overview: Tchetgen Tchetgen-Ying-Cui-Shi-Miao (2024, Statistical Science) frames PCI as replacing exact measured confounding with proxy bridge assumptions.
3. Completeness fragility: Canay-Santos-Shaikh (2013, Econometrica) shows nontrivial tests for completeness/identification can be impossible.
4. Partial identification anchor: Ghassami-Shpitser-Tchetgen (2023) explicitly drops completeness and derives proxy-based causal-effect bounds.
5. NPIV partial identification: Santos (2012, Econometrica) gives inference under partial identification; Santos (2011, JoE) and Severini-Tripathi (2012, JoE) address recoverable linear functionals.
6. Weakly identified functions: Bennett-Kallus-Mao-Newey-Syrgkanis-Uehara (2023) studies strongly identified functionals of weakly identified nuisance functions, including proximal examples.
7. Nonunique bridges: Zhang-Li-Miao-Tchetgen (2023, Statistics & Probability Letters) handles proximal inference without uniqueness assumptions.
8. Ill-posed inverse-problem econometrics: Darolles-Fan-Florens-Renault (2011, Econometrica), Florens-Johannes-Van Bellegem (2011, Econometric Theory), and Horowitz (2014, Annual Review of Economics) supply the spectral/regularization background.
9. Completeness conditions: Andrews (2017, Journal of Econometrics) and Hu-Shiu (2018, Econometric Theory) give sufficient examples/conditions, but not sharp failure-region ATE sets.
10. Many/panel proxies: Deaner (2018, Proxy Controls and Panel Data) and Deaner (2021, Many Proxy Controls) connect econometric proxy controls to rank and low-dimensional latent structure.
11. General partial-identification perspective: Kline-Tamer (2023, Annual Review of Economics) and Molinari (2020, Handbook/arXiv) motivate identified-set characterization rather than forced point identification.
12. Weak-IV analogy: Andrews-Stock-Sun (2019, Annual Review of Economics) suggests the right econometric template for weak-proxy robust inference.
13. Search note: the named Kompa-Sturma-Imbens NBER w34550 proximal paper was not verified in live search; current NBER/search results identify w34550 as an unrelated difference-in-differences/event-study survey.
prior_proposal_map:
1. Current qid eid_proximal_phase/v1 has only state and init pipeline stubs; no proposal/review content yet.
2. Closest proximal artifacts are pid_mediation_proxy and eid_frontdoor_multi_mediator, both about negative-control/proxy noncompleteness and sharp sets.
3. pid_mediation_proxy is the strongest warning: naive full bridge-interval sharpness was refuted and collapsed to a projection identity.
4. eid_frontdoor_multi_mediator supplies reusable shared-proxy/nonrectangular LP geometry, but sharp realization is the hard step.
5. q1_spectral_phase_transition is the best singular-value phase-transition analogue, though in panel/FWL rather than proximal bridges.
6. pid_dose_response_iv and pid_network_exposure contribute adjacent partial-ID pasting/nonrectangularity templates.
7. eid_lp_svar_nonequiv is less proximal but useful as a support-function separation template where the margin cannot be assumed.
8. Overall mined gap: replace assumed completeness or assumed margin by an observable operator/LP projection theorem with sharp regimes.
